description: A plain candid operational photograph (no on-image graphic design, slogans, or overlays). It shows a woman, viewed from behind, being escorted in handcuffs along a paved walkway/parking area outdoors under a partly cloudy sky. She wears a light tan/gray cardigan over dark slacks and heeled sandals, with her hair in a ponytail; her hands are cuffed behind her back. To her right, an officer grips her arm; he wears black tactical gear with a vest plate reading 'STATE TROOPER' and carries a radio and equipment on his belt. To the left in the background, another armed officer in tactical gear with a vest marked 'POLICE' walks ahead; vehicles and a tree are visible. Per the tweet, this depicts a woman taken into custody at the USCIS Orlando office by ICE and Florida Highway Patrol partners after an alleged marriage-fraud removal order from 14 years prior ('There will not be a happily ever after... there are consequences for breaking immigration law'). The image is a documentary arrest photo rather than a designed meme/poster; marked is_designed=false.
notable_text: (no on-image text; vest patches read 'STATE TROOPER' and 'POLICE')
